figures.caption: Table 3: Global-optimum attainment of the pipeline, with an idealized subproblem oracle and with real CVaR-QAOA on a single RTX 4080 GPU.

Figure 2: Runtime vs. problem scale. Brute-force scales as MN (measured; dashed extrapolation), while the pipeline is bounded by the slowest sub-QUBO’s QAOA plus a polynomial merge; the parallel variant (blue) runs sub-QUBOs concurrently. Past the crossover (MN ≈ 5 × 107) brute force becomes intractable.
Table 1: Main symbols used in the problem formulation.

Figure 1: End-to-end pipeline: an instruction is parsed by the fine-tuned LLM (SFT+DPO) into constraints; the constraint-aware graph is partitioned into qubit-budget subproblems; each subproblem is solved by CVaR-QAOA (W-state init + one-hot-preserving XY-mixer); per-subproblem profiles are recombined by a separator-DP merge.
